title: Product / data source vocabulary entry
description: A node in the EO product taxonomy. Nodes exist at varied levels of abstraction (component, service, mission, instrument, product, sub-product) and form a hierarchy via 'parent'. Content items reference a node by id from 'classification.products' or use-case 'steps[].products' at ANY level, uniformly. Owning service/mission is expressed structurally (walk 'parent'), not by a separate field.

name.description: Display name, e.g. 'European Ground Motion Service'.

level.description: Taxonomy layer (abstraction level) of this node, from coarse to fine. component: top-level grouping (e.g. Copernicus Services). service: a Copernicus service (CLMS, CAMS...). mission: a satellite mission (Sentinel-1...). instrument: a sensor on a mission (TROPOMI...). product: a distinct data/service product (EGMS, Sentinel-1 SLC, ERA5). sub_product: a variant/level of a product (EGMS Ortho, GLO-30). Depth is not fixed - branches can be 2 to 4 levels deep.

parent.description: Id of the single taxonomy parent (one layer up). Omit for root nodes. This is the structural hierarchy edge; it must resolve to another product id and must not form a cycle.

family.description: High-level grouping/branch for filtering and product pages.

nature.description: Whether outputs are direct observations, model output, derived/processed, or in-situ. Captures the modelled-vs-observed distinction (e.g. CAMS modelled vs Sentinel-5P/TROPOMI observational).

type.description: Optional free-text descriptive theme/kind, e.g. 'ground motion product', 'atmospheric reanalysis', 'DEM'. Descriptive only - the structural layer is 'level'.

aliases.description: Alternative names/acronyms to improve search (e.g. 'CLC' for CORINE Land Cover).

derived_from.description: Cross-branch provenance links to other product ids (NOT the taxonomy hierarchy). E.g. EGMS derived_from Sentinel-1. Each id must resolve to a product.

catalogue_metadata.description: Escape hatch for rich, source-specific catalogue fields that don't fit the core taxonomy fields above (e.g. region/coverage, spatial/temporal resolution, processing level, access method, file format, date coverage, user manual link). Populated when importing from a detailed product catalogue (e.g. a CMEMS/CLMS/CAMS/CEMS/C3S product list) rather than hand-authored. Keep keys descriptive; this is intentionally open since catalogue exports vary by source. If a key here turns out to be useful for filtering/display across the site, promote it to a real top-level field instead.
